audit_status.source_base: Hebrew 2 Samuel chapter 19, first-pass draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Joab's coercive response to grief, army shame, political return campaign, Amasa appointment, Shimei's apology and execution proposal, Mephibosheth's accessibility testimony and partial judgment, Barzillai's age and self-assessment, Chimham patronage, tribal rivalry, and threatened renewed fracture.

verses.restored: It was told Joab, Look, king weeps and mourns over Absalom.
Rescue/victory on that day became mourning for all people, because people heard that day, King is grieved over his son.
People entered city stealthily that day, as people ashamed steal away when they flee in battle.
King covered his face, and king cried with a great voice, My son Absalom, Absalom my son, my son.
Joab came to king at house and said, Today you have shamed faces of all your servants who rescued your life today, lives of your sons and daughters, lives of your wives, and lives of your concubines.

By loving those who hate you and hating those who love you, you declared today that commanders and servants are nothing to you. Today I know that if Absalom lived and all of us died, then it would be right in your eyes.

Now arise, go out, and speak to heart of your servants. By YHWH I swear, if you do not go out, not a man will remain with you tonight, and this will be worse for you than all evil that came upon you from youth until now.
King arose and sat in gate. They told all people, saying, Look, king sits in gate. All people came before king. Israel had fled, each to his tent.
All people disputed in all tribes of Israel, saying, King rescued us from hand of enemies and delivered us from hand of Philistines, but now he fled from land before Absalom.
Absalom, whom we anointed over us, died in battle. Now why are you silent about returning king?
King David sent to Zadok and Abiathar the priests, saying, Speak to elders of Judah: Why are you last to return king to his house, when word of all Israel has come to king at his house?
You are my brothers; you are my bone and my flesh. Why are you last to return king?
Say to Amasa, Are you not my bone and flesh? Thus may Divine do to me and thus add if you are not commander of army before me all days in place of Joab.
He turned heart of every man of Judah as one man, and they sent to king, Return, you and all your servants.
King returned and came as far as Jordan. Judah came to Gilgal to meet king, to bring king across Jordan.
Shimei son of Gera, Benjaminite from Bahurim, hurried and came down with men of Judah to meet king David.
With him were one thousand men from Benjamin, and Ziba servant of house of Saul with his fifteen sons and twenty servants. They rushed to Jordan before king.
The crossing service/ferry passed to carry household of king and do what was good in his eyes. Shimei son of Gera fell before king as he crossed Jordan.
He said to king, Do not let my lord reckon guilt to me. Do not remember what your servant did perversely on day my lord king went out from Jerusalem. Do not let king set it on his heart.
Your servant knows I sinned. Look, I came today first from all house of Joseph to go down to meet my lord king.
Abishai son of Zeruiah answered and said, Shall Shimei not be put to death for this, because he cursed anointed of YHWH?
David said, What is there to me and to you, sons of Zeruiah, that you become an adversary to me today? Shall any man be put to death today in Israel? Do I not know that today I am king over Israel?
King said to Shimei, You shall not die. King swore to him.
Mephibosheth son of Saul came down to meet king. He had not attended to his feet/legs, made his mustache, or washed his clothes from day king went until day he returned in peace.
When he came to Jerusalem to meet king, king said to him, Why did you not go with me, Mephibosheth?
He said, My lord king, my servant deceived me. Your servant said, I will saddle donkey for myself and ride upon it and go with king, because your servant is disabled in his feet.
He slandered your servant to my lord king. My lord king is like messenger of Divine; do what is good in your eyes.
All house of my father were men of death before my lord king, yet you placed your servant among those eating at your table. What further right do I have to cry to king?
King said to him, Why speak any more of your matters? I say, you and Ziba shall divide land.
Mephibosheth said to king, Let him take even all, after my lord king has come in peace to his house.
Barzillai the Gileadite came down from Rogelim and crossed Jordan with king, to send him across Jordan.
Barzillai was very old, eighty years old. He had sustained king during his stay at Mahanaim, because he was a very great/wealthy man.
King said to Barzillai, Cross with me, and I will sustain you with me in Jerusalem.
Barzillai said to king, How many are days of years of my life, that I should go up with king to Jerusalem?

I am eighty years old today. Can I discern between good and evil? Can your servant taste what I eat or drink? Can I still hear voice of singing men and women? Why should your servant become a burden to my lord king?
Your servant will cross Jordan a little with king. Why should king repay me with this reward?
Please let your servant return, that I may die in my city near grave of my father and mother. Look, your servant Chimham may cross with my lord king; do for him what is good in your eyes.
King said, Chimham shall cross with me, and I will do for him what is good in your eyes. All you choose upon me I will do for you.
All people crossed Jordan, and king crossed. King kissed Barzillai and blessed him, and he returned to his place.
King crossed to Gilgal, and Chimham crossed with him. All people of Judah and half people of Israel brought king across.
Look, every man of Israel came to king and said, Why did our brothers, men of Judah, steal you and bring king, his household, and all men of David with him across Jordan?
Every man of Judah answered men of Israel, Because king is near to us. Why does this anger you? Have we eaten from king or received a gift?

Men of Israel answered men of Judah, We have ten shares in king, and also in David more than you. Why did you treat us lightly, so our word was not first in returning our king? Word of men of Judah was fiercer than word of men of Israel.

translation_notes: Joab responds to David's grief with political threat and shame. A ruler has duties to surviving people, but bereavement does not become illegitimate because it disrupts victory celebration.
The soldiers' lives matter independently of their usefulness to David. Joab's claim that David loves enemies and hates friends simplifies a father's grief into political betrayal.
David uses priests to lobby Judah and offers Amasa Joab's command, blending kinship, patronage, and institutional religious networks in his return campaign.
Shimei apologizes under a thousand-person political delegation. David's refusal to execute him is restraint; 'anointed' language still does not make criticism treason.
Mephibosheth's unkempt body and clothes are signs of mourning/protest, not neglect proving incompetence.
His testimony makes accessibility explicit: he needed a saddled animal, and Ziba controlled the assistance. David again refuses a full hearing and splits property despite conflicting accounts.
Mephibosheth's willingness to surrender all land may reflect relief, dependence, pressure, or rhetorical loyalty; it should not validate the unjust judgment.
Barzillai evaluates his own age, senses, preferences, burial wishes, and travel capacity. His self-description must not become a universal stereotype about older adults.
He declines palace care and chooses home, family grave, and succession support for Chimham. Autonomy includes refusing prestigious arrangements.
The chapter ends in fierce Judah-Israel rivalry, showing that royal restoration did not heal structural division.
safety_note: Do not shame grief, execute critics, or decide disability disputes without accessible testimony and evidence. Do not treat older people as burdens or assume palace care is best. Restored leadership must hear those harmed by its earlier emergency judgments and address regional exclusion before it becomes renewed conflict.
